perturbation.original_text: NON -COMPETITION AND NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT AGREEMENT (this "Agreement"), dated as of May 4, 2004, by and between FREESTAR TECHNOLOGY CORPORATION, a Nevada corporation having offices at Calle Fantino Falco, J.A. Baez Building, 2nd Floor, Santo Domingo, Dominican Republic (the "Buyer"), and UNICOMP, INC., a Colorado corporation having offices at 6478 Putnam Ford Road, Suite 208, Woodstock, Georgia 30189 (the "Stockholder");
perturbation.changed_text: NON -COMPETITION AND NON-DISCLOSURE PACT (this "Agreement"), dated as of May 4, 2004, by and between FREESTAR TECHNOLOGY CORPORATION, a Nevada corporation having offices at Calle Fantino Falco, J.A. Baez Building, 2nd Floor, Santo Domingo, Dominican Republic (the "Purchaser"), and UNICOMP, INC., a Colorado corporation having offices at 6478 Putnam Ford Road, Suite 208, Woodstock, Georgia 30189 (the "Shareholder");
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Agreement' to 'Pact,' 'Buyer' to 'Purchaser,' and 'Stockholder' to 'Shareholder' creates ambiguity as to whether these terms are interchangeable. While context hints at it, the contract never explicitly states the equivalency. The introduction's definition of 'Buyer' is changed to 'Purchaser', and 'Stockholder' is now called 'Shareholder', leading to potential confusion later in the document when 'Buyer' and 'Stockholder' are used, creating an in-text contradiction regarding who is being reffered to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, on the date hereof, pursuant to that certain Asset Purchase Agreement of even date herewith (the "Purchase Agreement"), by and among the Buyer, UniPay, Inc. (the "Seller") and the Stockholder, the Buyer is acquiring all of the assets and properties of the Seller used to provide or act as an electronic gateway between sellers of goods and services and processors of payments for such goods and services made via credit cards and debit cards, presently located at Seller's facility in Murphy, North Carolina (the "Business"), as a going concern; WHEREAS, the Stockholder has heretofore been the sole stockholder of the Seller, and as such, will derive substantial benefit from the transactions contemplated by the Purchase Agreement; WHEREAS, by reason of its ownership and management of the Seller, the Stockholder has detailed knowledge and possesses confidential information concerning the Business; WHEREAS, in order to induce the Buyer to consummate the transactions contemplated by the Purchase Agreement, the Stockholder has agreed, and the Buyer has required the Stockholder, to enter into this Agreement;
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes the defined terms 'Buyer' to 'Purchaser' and 'Stockholder' to 'Shareholder'. The contradicted text uses the original terms 'Buyer' and 'Stockholder' throughout the WHEREAS clauses. Since the contract doesn't explicitly state that 'Buyer' and 'Purchaser', or 'Stockholder' and 'Shareholder' are interchangeable, this creates ambiguity and a potential contradiction. A reader wouldn't know which entity is being referred to, because the definition was changed, and later it uses the original defined term.

perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS, on the date hereof, pursuant to that certain Asset Purchase Agreement of even date herewith (the "Purchase Agreement"), by and among the Buyer, UniPay, Inc. (the "Seller") and the Stockholder, the Buyer is acquiring all of the assets and properties of the Seller used to provide or act as an electronic gateway between sellers of goods and services and processors of payments for such goods and services made via credit cards and debit cards, presently located at Seller's facility in Murphy, North Carolina (the "Business"), as a going concern;
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS, on the date hereof, pursuant to that certain Asset Purchase Accord of even date herewith (the "Purchase Agreement"), by and among the Purchaser, UniPay, Inc. (the "Seller") and the Shareholder, the Buyer is acquiring all of the assets and properties of the Seller used to provide or act as an electronic gateway between sellers of goods and services and processors of payments for such goods and services made via credit cards and debit cards, presently located at Seller's facility in Murphy, North Carolina (the "Business"), as a going concern;
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Agreement' to 'Accord' and using the previously defined 'Purchaser' and 'Shareholder' while still having 'Buyer' in the same sentence. The 'Buyer' is referenced in the sentence although the initial definition now calls them 'Purchaser'. The 'Stockholder' is referenced as 'Shareholder' in the first part of the phrase but the second reference calls them 'Stockholder', creating an in-text contradiction because the words are being used in the same sentence. This contradiction also affects the enforcement because there is uncertainty in which one to use, since they are within the same phrase.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, on the date hereof, pursuant to that certain Asset Purchase Accord of even date herewith (the "Purchase Agreement"), by and among the Purchaser, UniPay, Inc. (the "Seller") and the Shareholder, the Buyer is acquiring all of the assets and properties of the Seller used to provide or act as an electronic gateway between sellers of goods and services and processors of payments for such goods and services made via credit cards and debit cards, presently located at Seller's facility in Murphy, North Carolina (the "Business"), as a going concern;
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Agreement' to 'Accord' and the inconsistent use of 'Buyer'/'Purchaser' and 'Stockholder'/'Shareholder' within the same sentence, while the initial definition defines them as 'Purchaser' and 'Shareholder' respectively, creates a direct in-text contradiction. This affects enforceability due to uncertainty in which term to use, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation. The Changed Text and Contradicted Text are identical, but the modification introduces a conflict with the initial definitions, causing a contradiction as described by the Misaligned Terminology definition.

perturbation.original_text: Restrictive Covenants. (a) The Stockholder hereby acknowledges and agrees that: (i) the business contacts, customers, suppliers, technology, know- how, trade secrets, marketing techniques, operating methods and other aspects of the Business have been of value to the Seller, and have provided the Seller (and will hereafter provide the Buyer) with substantial competitive advantage in the operation of the Business, and (ii) by virtue of its ownership and management of the Seller as its sole stockholder, the Stockholder has detailed knowledge of and possesses confidential information concerning the Business.
perturbation.changed_text: Restrictive Covenants. (a) The Investor hereby acknowledges and agrees that: (i) the business contacts, customers, suppliers, technology, know- how, trade secrets, marketing techniques, operating methods and other aspects of the Enterprise have been of value to the Seller, and have provided the Seller (and will hereafter provide the Buyer) with substantial competitive advantage in the operation of the Business, and (ii) by virtue of its ownership and management of the Seller as its sole stockholder, the Investor has detailed knowledge of and possesses confidential information concerning the Business.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Stockholder' to 'Investor' and 'Business' to 'Enterprise' in this section creates confusion. The 'Stockholder' definition in the intro was changed to 'Shareholder', so now we're calling them 'Investor' in section 10(a) and that is not defined. Also, 'Enterprise' instead of 'Business' makes it ambiguous as to the scope of the non-compete agreement, because it is unclear that the new term 'Enterprise' includes the 'Business', creating an in-text contradiction regarding the scope of the non-compete.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Investor hereby acknowledges and agrees that: (i) the business contacts, customers, suppliers, technology, know- how, trade secrets, marketing techniques, operating methods and other aspects of the Enterprise have been of value to the Seller, and have provided the Seller (and will hereafter provide the Buyer) with substantial competitive advantage in the operation of the Business, and (ii) by virtue of its ownership and management of the Seller as its sole stockholder, the Investor has detailed knowledge of and possesses confidential information concerning the Business.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'Stockholder' to 'Investor' and 'Business' to 'Enterprise'. Since the 'Stockholder' is defined as 'Shareholder' elsewhere, using 'Investor' creates a misalignment because it is not defined, leading to ambiguity about who is bound by the restrictive covenants. Furthermore, changing 'Business' to 'Enterprise' introduces ambiguity regarding the scope of the non-compete agreement, because the contradicted text refers to 'Enterprise', so it is not clear whether it includes the 'Business' as originally intended. This directly contradicts the original intent and scope of the restrictive covenants, thereby creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (b) The Stockholder hereby agrees, for the benefit of the Buyer and its subsidiaries, that it shall not, directly or indirectly, for itself or through or on behalf of any of its subsidiaries or affiliates, or any other person or entity, in each case relating only to the Business: (i) at any time from and after the date hereof, except to the extent required by law or in connection with any legal proceeding of which the Stockholder has theretofore given written notice to the Buyer, divulge, transmit or otherwise disclose or cause to be divulged, transmitted or otherwise disclosed, any business contacts, client or customer lists, technology, know-how, trade secrets, marketing techniques, contracts or other confidential or proprietary information of the Seller or the Buyer of whatever nature, whether existing on or prior to the date hereof or arising from and after the date hereof (provided, however, that for purposes hereof, information shall not be considered to be confidential or proprietary if (A) it is a matter of common knowledge or public record, (B) it is generally known in the industry, or (C) the Stockholder can demonstrate that such information was already known to the recipient thereof other than by reason of any breach of any obligation under this Agreement or any other confidentiality or non- disclosure agreement known to the Stockholder);
perturbation.changed_text: (b) The Investor hereby agrees, for the benefit of the Purchaser and its subsidiaries, that it shall not, directly or indirectly, for itself or through or on behalf of any of its subsidiaries or affiliates, or any other person or entity, in each case relating only to the Enterprise: (i) at any time from and after the date hereof, except to the extent required by law or in connection with any legal proceeding of which the Shareholder has theretofore given written notice to the Purchaser, divulge, transmit or otherwise disclose or cause to be divulged, transmitted or otherwise disclosed, any business contacts, client or customer lists, technology, know-how, trade secrets, marketing techniques, contracts or other confidential or proprietary information of the Seller or the Buyer of whatever nature, whether existing on or prior to the date hereof or arising from and after the date hereof (provided, however, that for purposes hereof, information shall not be considered to be confidential or proprietary if (A) it is a matter of common knowledge or public record, (B) it is generally known in the industry, or (C) the Stockholder can demonstrate that such information was already known to the recipient thereof other than by reason of any breach of any obligation under this Agreement or any other confidentiality or non- disclosure agreement known to the Stockholder);
perturbation.explanation: Using 'Investor' instead of 'Stockholder', 'Purchaser' instead of 'Buyer', and 'Enterprise' instead of 'Business,' while also keeping some original definitions causes an in-text contradiction. In this part of the contract, we define the party as 'Investor', which creates confusion, since the original definition was changed to 'Shareholder'. Also, The original contract defines 'Stockholder' and 'Buyer' to be the parties involved in the restrictive covenant. The last sentence calls out 'Stockholder', which is contradictory to the use of 'Investor' and 'Purchaser' in the beginning of section 10(b). 'Enterprise' is used, but then the next sentence still says 'Business', creating a contradiction between these terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) The Investor hereby agrees, for the benefit of the Purchaser and its subsidiaries, that it shall not, directly or indirectly, for itself or through or on behalf of any of its subsidiaries or affiliates, or any other person or entity, in each case relating only to the Enterprise: (i) at any time from and after the date hereof, except to the extent required by law or in connection with any legal proceeding of which the Shareholder has theretofore given written notice to the Purchaser, divulge, transmit or otherwise disclose or cause to be divulged, transmitted or otherwise disclosed, any business contacts, client or customer lists, technology, know-how, trade secrets, marketing techniques, contracts or other confidential or proprietary information of the Seller or the Buyer of whatever nature, whether existing on or prior to the date hereof or arising from and after the date hereof (provided, however, that for purposes hereof, information shall not be considered to be confidential or proprietary if (A) it is a matter of common knowledge or public record, (B) it is generally known in the industry, or (C) the Stockholder can demonstrate that such information was already known to the recipient thereof other than by reason of any breach of any obligation under this Agreement or any other confidentiality or non- disclosure agreement known to the Stockholder);
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces 'Investor', 'Purchaser', and 'Enterprise' while keeping 'Stockholder' and 'Business' in subsequent clauses. This creates a misaligned terminology and in-text contradiction according to the provided definition. The contradictory terms within the same clause causes confusion and makes the contract internally inconsistent. Therefore the changed text causes in-text contradiction to the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Remedies. The Stockholder and the Buyer hereby acknowledge and agree that any breach by the Stockholder, directly or indirectly, of the foregoing restrictive covenants will cause the Buyer and/or its affiliates irreparable injury for which there is no adequate remedy at law. Accordingly, the Stockholder expressly agrees that, in the event of any such breach or any threatened breach hereunder by the Stockholder, directly or indirectly, the Buyer and its subsidiaries shall be entitled, in addition to any and all other remedies available, to seek and obtain injunctive and/or other equitable relief to require specific performance of or prevent, restrain and/or enjoin a breach under the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Remedies. The Shareholder and the Purchaser hereby acknowledge and agree that any breach by the Investor, directly or indirectly, of the foregoing restrictive covenants will cause the Purchaser and/or its affiliates irreparable injury for which there is no adequate remedy at law. Accordingly, the Stockholder expressly agrees that, in the event of any such breach or any threatened breach hereunder by the Investor, directly or indirectly, the Purchaser and its subsidiaries shall be entitled, in addition to any and all other remedies available, to seek and obtain injunctive and/or other equitable relief to require specific performance of or prevent, restrain and/or enjoin a breach under the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Here 'Stockholder' is interchanged with 'Shareholder', 'Purchaser' and 'Investor'. The remedies section is now contradictory. It starts by using 'Shareholder' and 'Purchaser', but then references 'Investor' and 'Stockholder'. If the parties breach, there is no clarification on who to penalize between the four different titles. Also, the definitions were not clear on if they were referencing the same legal entity. The text contradicts itself by using 'Stockholder', even though the previous usage was 'Shareholder' and 'Investor'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Shareholder and the Purchaser hereby acknowledge and agree that any breach by the Investor, directly or indirectly, of the foregoing restrictive covenants will cause the Purchaser and/or its affiliates irreparable injury for which there is no adequate remedy at law. Accordingly, the Stockholder expressly agrees that, in the event of any such breach or any threatened breach hereunder by the Investor, directly or indirectly, the Purchaser and its subsidiaries shall be entitled, in addition to any and all other remedies available, to seek and obtain injunctive and/or other equitable relief to require specific performance of or prevent, restrain and/or enjoin a breach under the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces multiple terms ('Shareholder', 'Purchaser', 'Investor', 'Stockholder') to refer to parties involved in the agreement without clear definitions. This contradicts itself, as the remedies section now references 'Investor' and 'Stockholder', while the initial acknowledgment uses 'Shareholder' and 'Purchaser.' This creates ambiguity about who is responsible for a breach, causing a strong in-text contradiction as per the Misaligned Terminology definition, making enforcement unclear.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Benefits and Obligations. This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of and shall be enforceable by the Buyer and its subsidiaries, successors and assigns, and the Stockholder and its subsidiaries, successors and assigns; provided, however, that the obligations of the Stockholder contained herein may not be delegated or assigned.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Benefits and Obligations. This Pact shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of and shall be enforceable by the Purchaser and its subsidiaries, successors and assigns, and the Shareholder and its subsidiaries, successors and assigns; provided, however, that the obligations of the Investor contained herein may not be delegated or assigned.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 4, the contract is changed to 'Pact', 'Purchaser', 'Shareholder', and 'Investor', while the initial definitions and previous use have been inconsistent. The use of the terms 'Pact', 'Purchaser', and 'Shareholder' were the initial definitions, but the change to 'Investor' here is not consistent with the prior changed terms. This creates an internal contradiction regarding which party ('Purchaser', 'Shareholder', or 'Investor') is subject to the non-delegation clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Pact shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of and shall be enforceable by the Purchaser and its subsidiaries, successors and assigns, and the Shareholder and its subsidiaries, successors and assigns; provided, however, that the obligations of the Investor contained herein may not be delegated or assigned.
perturbation.justification: The explanation states that the changed text replaces 'Buyer' with 'Purchaser', 'Stockholder' with 'Shareholder', but then inconsistently uses 'Investor' instead of keeping 'Shareholder' or 'Purchaser'. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, meaning the contradiction is directly present: the non-delegation clause applies to the 'Investor', even though the contract has been changed to consistently use 'Purchaser' and 'Shareholder' which is contradictory to the definition given for 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' that uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.
